{{出典の明記|date=2020年9月}} This article treats '''Kumano Shrine''' (くまのじんじゃ) in {{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794|1=Takashima, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, which is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Although named Kumano Shrine, there is also a tradition that it is unrelated to the so-called {{ill|lt=Kumano Sanzan|Q10403700|WD=Q10403700|1=Kumano Three Shrines}}. Formerly, three major routes ran north-south through Takashima District: the Hokurikudō (Nishi-Ōmi road), Akatsuki (Meigetsu) road, and Saba road. Of these, the Meigetsu road ran across Kumano-yama (today's Aibano upland). It is said Oda Nobunaga used this road during his campaign against the Asakura. Local priestly-family documents (e.g., ''Furumiya Family Documents,'' ''Aiba Family Documents'') record that "the shikinaisha Kumano Shrine originally stood at Nos e in Kumano-yama along Meigetsu road; on March 16, Genki 3, it was burned by Mori Hanbei Tame-mura, and residents moved the sacred symbol into the precinct of {{ill|lt=Harifu Shrine|Q11555577|WD=Q11555577|1=Hanifu Shrine}}." The old site name Mikoshi-daira remains, but it is now within a Self-Defense Force training area and inaccessible. At the former south end (Koga village) and north end (Nishino village) of Meigetsu road were remote worship sites of Kumano Shrine; these correspond to the present Kumano Shrines at Kamikoga in Azumigawa-chō and at Oi in Imazu-chō. === Kumano Shrine [sessha of Harifu Shrine] === {{神社 | 名称=Kumano Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Takashima-kumano1.JPG|250px]]
Kumano Shrine, sessha of Harifu Shrine | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794|1=Takashima, Shiga}}Shin-Asahi-chō Aiba 3363 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E|}} | 祭神=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
sessha of {{ill|lt=Harifu Shrine|Q11555577|WD=Q11555577|1=Hanifu Shrine}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524|1=April 20}} | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kumano Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto}} === History === It is said to have been moved into Harifu Shrine's precinct by residents of Kumano-yama in the Tenshō era. Until early Meiji, a Kumano festival was held on June 6 with Noh performances. === Festival === * Annual festival: April 20 === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Takesusa Shrine (Takashima)|Q11387238|WD=Q11387238|lt=Takesusa Shrine|1=Tatehaya Shrine (Takashima)}} * Former Kizu port site * Kizu ancient iron-production site * Shinsei Chemical Industry Shin-Asahi Plant * NPO Kumano-yama Net * Wakamiya Hachiman-sha * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi Shrine (Takashima)|Q11433666|WD=Q11433666|lt=Ōkuninushi Shrine|1=Ookuninushi Shrine (Takashima)}} === Kumano Shrine [Kamikoga, Azumigawa-chō] === {{神社 | 名称=Kumano Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Takashima-kumano2.JPG|250px]]
Kumano Shrine (Kamikoga) honden | 所在地=Kamikoga 1391, Azumigawa-chō, Takashima, Shiga | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|22|06|N|135|58|22|E|}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} | 神事= | 地図={{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} === History === Shrine tradition says it was rebuilt in Kōnin 14. In old times it was called '''Hakusan-gū''', but in Meiji it changed to the present name. In Meiji 9 it was ranked village shrine. === Festival === * Annual festival: May 1 === Nearby === * Genshō-ji * Entsū-ji * Kiyomizu-dera * Hie Shrine * Kamikoga Hachiman Shrine * Azumigawa Koga Post Office === Kumano Shrine [Oi, Imazu-chō] === {{神社 | 名称=Kumano Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Takashima-kumano3.JPG|250px]]
Kumano Shrine (Oi) approach | 所在地=Oi 742, Imazu-chō, Takashima, Shiga | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|24|17|N|135|59|17|E|}} | 祭神=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} | 神事= | 地図={{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === It formerly stood in Nishino village and was moved to the current location in the Eishō era. It is currently outside animal-damage-prevention fencing, so ordinary worship is generally not possible. === Festival === * Annual festival: April 29 === Nearby=== * Saikō-ji * {{ill|lt=Yuge Shrine (Takashima)|Q11487078|WD=Q11487078|lt=Yuge Shrine|1=Yuge Shrine (Takashima)}} * Imazu General Sports Park === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shinto|Q812767|WD=Q812767|1=Shinto}} * {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine|Q3200355|WD=Q3200355|1=Kumano shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くまのしんしやたかしまし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kumano Shrine (Q11568963)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kumano Shrine (Q11568963)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKumano Shrine (Q11568963)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKumano Shrine (Q11568963)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kumano Shrine (Q11568963)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{出典の明記|date=2020年9月}} This article treats '''Kumano Shrine''' (くまのじんじゃ) in {{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794|1=Takashima, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, which is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Although named Kumano Shrine, there is also a tradition that it is unrelated to the so-called {{ill|lt=Kumano Sanzan|Q10403700|WD=Q10403700|1=Kumano Three Shrines}}. Formerly, three major routes ran north-south through Takashima District: the Hokurikudō (Nishi-Ōmi road), Akatsuki (Meigetsu) road, and Saba road. Of these, the Meigetsu road ran across Kumano-yama (today's Aibano upland). It is said Oda Nobunaga used this road during his campaign against the Asakura. Local priestly-family documents (e.g., ''Furumiya Family Documents,'' ''Aiba Family Documents'') record that "the shikinaisha Kumano Shrine originally stood at Nos e in Kumano-yama along Meigetsu road; on March 16, Genki 3, it was burned by Mori Hanbei Tame-mura, and residents moved the sacred symbol into the precinct of {{ill|lt=Harifu Shrine|Q11555577|WD=Q11555577|1=Hanifu Shrine}}." The old site name Mikoshi-daira remains, but it is now within a Self-Defense Force training area and inaccessible. At the former south end (Koga village) and north end (Nishino village) of Meigetsu road were remote worship sites of Kumano Shrine; these correspond to the present Kumano Shrines at Kamikoga in Azumigawa-chō and at Oi in Imazu-chō. === Kumano Shrine [sessha of Harifu Shrine] === {{神社 | 名称=Kumano Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Takashima-kumano1.JPG|250px]]
Kumano Shrine, sessha of Harifu Shrine | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794|1=Takashima, Shiga}}Shin-Asahi-chō Aiba 3363 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E|}} | 祭神=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
sessha of {{ill|lt=Harifu Shrine|Q11555577|WD=Q11555577|1=Hanifu Shrine}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524|1=April 20}} | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kumano Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto}} === History === It is said to have been moved into Harifu Shrine's precinct by residents of Kumano-yama in the Tenshō era. Until early Meiji, a Kumano festival was held on June 6 with Noh performances. === Festival === * Annual festival: April 20 === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Takesusa Shrine (Takashima)|Q11387238|WD=Q11387238|lt=Takesusa Shrine|1=Tatehaya Shrine (Takashima)}} * Former Kizu port site * Kizu ancient iron-production site * Shinsei Chemical Industry Shin-Asahi Plant * NPO Kumano-yama Net * Wakamiya Hachiman-sha * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi Shrine (Takashima)|Q11433666|WD=Q11433666|lt=Ōkuninushi Shrine|1=Ookuninushi Shrine (Takashima)}} === Kumano Shrine [Kamikoga, Azumigawa-chō] === {{神社 | 名称=Kumano Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Takashima-kumano2.JPG|250px]]
Kumano Shrine (Kamikoga) honden | 所在地=Kamikoga 1391, Azumigawa-chō, Takashima, Shiga | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|22|06|N|135|58|22|E|}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} | 神事= | 地図={{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} === History === Shrine tradition says it was rebuilt in Kōnin 14. In old times it was called '''Hakusan-gū''', but in Meiji it changed to the present name. In Meiji 9 it was ranked village shrine. === Festival === * Annual festival: May 1 === Nearby === * Genshō-ji * Entsū-ji * Kiyomizu-dera * Hie Shrine * Kamikoga Hachiman Shrine * Azumigawa Koga Post Office === Kumano Shrine [Oi, Imazu-chō] === {{神社 | 名称=Kumano Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Takashima-kumano3.JPG|250px]]
Kumano Shrine (Oi) approach | 所在地=Oi 742, Imazu-chō, Takashima, Shiga | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|24|17|N|135|59|17|E|}} | 祭神=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} | 神事= | 地図={{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === It formerly stood in Nishino village and was moved to the current location in the Eishō era. It is currently outside animal-damage-prevention fencing, so ordinary worship is generally not possible. === Festival === * Annual festival: April 29 === Nearby=== * Saikō-ji * {{ill|lt=Yuge Shrine (Takashima)|Q11487078|WD=Q11487078|lt=Yuge Shrine|1=Yuge Shrine (Takashima)}} * Imazu General Sports Park === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shinto|Q812767|WD=Q812767|1=Shinto}} * {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine|Q3200355|WD=Q3200355|1=Kumano shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くまのしんしやたかしまし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kumano Shrine (Q11568963)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kumano Shrine (Q11568963)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QKumano Shrine (Q11568963)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKumano Shrine (Q11568963)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kumano Shrine (Q11568963)}}[[Category:qq]]

{{出典の明記|date=2020年9月}} This article treats '''Kumano Shrine''' (くまのじんじゃ) in {{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794|1=Takashima, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, which is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Although named Kumano Shrine, there is also a tradition that it is unrelated to the so-called {{ill|lt=Kumano Sanzan|Q10403700|WD=Q10403700|1=Kumano Three Shrines}}. Formerly, three major routes ran north-south through Takashima District: the Hokurikudō (Nishi-Ōmi road), Akatsuki (Meigetsu) road, and Saba road. Of these, the Meigetsu road ran across Kumano-yama (today's Aibano upland). It is said Oda Nobunaga used this road during his campaign against the Asakura. Local priestly-family documents (e.g., ''Furumiya Family Documents,'' ''Aiba Family Documents'') record that "the shikinaisha Kumano Shrine originally stood at Nos e in Kumano-yama along Meigetsu road; on March 16, Genki 3, it was burned by Mori Hanbei Tame-mura, and residents moved the sacred symbol into the precinct of {{ill|lt=Harifu Shrine|Q11555577|WD=Q11555577|1=Hanifu Shrine}}." The old site name Mikoshi-daira remains, but it is now within a Self-Defense Force training area and inaccessible. At the former south end (Koga village) and north end (Nishino village) of Meigetsu road were remote worship sites of Kumano Shrine; these correspond to the present Kumano Shrines at Kamikoga in Azumigawa-chō and at Oi in Imazu-chō. === Kumano Shrine [sessha of Harifu Shrine] === {{神社 | 名称=Kumano Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Takashima-kumano1.JPG|250px]]
Kumano Shrine, sessha of Harifu Shrine | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794|1=Takashima, Shiga}}Shin-Asahi-chō Aiba 3363 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E|}} | 祭神=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
sessha of {{ill|lt=Harifu Shrine|Q11555577|WD=Q11555577|1=Hanifu Shrine}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524|1=April 20}} | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kumano Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto}} === History === It is said to have been moved into Harifu Shrine's precinct by residents of Kumano-yama in the Tenshō era. Until early Meiji, a Kumano festival was held on June 6 with Noh performances. === Festival === * Annual festival: April 20 === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Takesusa Shrine (Takashima)|Q11387238|WD=Q11387238|lt=Takesusa Shrine|1=Tatehaya Shrine (Takashima)}} * Former Kizu port site * Kizu ancient iron-production site * Shinsei Chemical Industry Shin-Asahi Plant * NPO Kumano-yama Net * Wakamiya Hachiman-sha * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi Shrine (Takashima)|Q11433666|WD=Q11433666|lt=Ōkuninushi Shrine|1=Ookuninushi Shrine (Takashima)}} === Kumano Shrine [Kamikoga, Azumigawa-chō] === {{神社 | 名称=Kumano Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Takashima-kumano2.JPG|250px]]
Kumano Shrine (Kamikoga) honden | 所在地=Kamikoga 1391, Azumigawa-chō, Takashima, Shiga | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|22|06|N|135|58|22|E|}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}

Formerly, three major routes ran north-south through Takashima District: the Hokurikudō (Nishi-Ōmi road), Akatsuki (Meigetsu) road, and Saba road. Of these, the Meigetsu road ran across Kumano-yama (today's Aibano upland). It is said Oda Nobunaga used this road during his campaign against the Asakura. Local priestly-family documents (e.g., ''Furumiya Family Documents,'' ''Aiba Family Documents'') record that "the shikinaisha Kumano Shrine originally stood at Nos e in Kumano-yama along Meigetsu road; on March 16, Genki 3, it was burned by Mori Hanbei Tame-mura, and residents moved the sacred symbol into the precinct of {{ill|lt=Harifu Shrine|Q11555577|WD=Q11555577|1=Hanifu Shrine}}." The old site name Mikoshi-daira remains, but it is now within a Self-Defense Force training area and inaccessible. At the former south end (Koga village) and north end (Nishino village) of Meigetsu road were remote worship sites of Kumano Shrine; these correspond to the present Kumano Shrines at Kamikoga in Azumigawa-chō and at Oi in Imazu-chō. === Kumano Shrine [sessha of Harifu Shrine] === {{神社 | 名称=Kumano Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Takashima-kumano1.JPG|250px]]

{{出典の明記|date=2020年9月}} This article treats '''Kumano Shrine''' (くまのじんじゃ) in {{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794|1=Takashima, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, which is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Although named Kumano Shrine, there is also a tradition that it is unrelated to the so-called {{ill|lt=Kumano Sanzan|Q10403700|WD=Q10403700|1=Kumano Three Shrines}}. Formerly, three major routes ran north-south through Takashima District: the Hokurikudō (Nishi-Ōmi road), Akatsuki (Meigetsu) road, and Saba road. Of these, the Meigetsu road ran across Kumano-yama (today's Aibano upland). It is said Oda Nobunaga used this road during his campaign against the Asakura. Local priestly-family documents (e.g., ''Furumiya Family Documents,'' ''Aiba Family Documents'') record that "the shikinaisha Kumano Shrine originally stood at Nos e in Kumano-yama along Meigetsu road; on March 16, Genki 3, it was burned by Mori Hanbei Tame-mura, and residents moved the sacred symbol into the precinct of {{ill|lt=Harifu Shrine|Q11555577|WD=Q11555577|1=Hanifu Shrine}}." The old site name Mikoshi-daira remains, but it is now within a Self-Defense Force training area and inaccessible. At the former south end (Koga village) and north end (Nishino village) of Meigetsu road were remote worship sites of Kumano Shrine; these correspond to the present Kumano Shrines at Kamikoga in Azumigawa-chō and at Oi in Imazu-chō. === Kumano Shrine [sessha of Harifu Shrine] === {{神社 | 名称=Kumano Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Takashima-kumano1.JPG|250px]]
Kumano Shrine, sessha of Harifu Shrine | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|WD=Q845794|1=Takashima, Shiga}}Shin-Asahi-chō Aiba 3363 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E|}} | 祭神=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
sessha of {{ill|lt=Harifu Shrine|Q11555577|WD=Q11555577|1=Hanifu Shrine}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524|1=April 20}} | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kumano Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto}} === History === It is said to have been moved into Harifu Shrine's precinct by residents of Kumano-yama in the Tenshō era. Until early Meiji, a Kumano festival was held on June 6 with Noh performances. === Festival === * Annual festival: April 20 === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Takesusa Shrine (Takashima)|Q11387238|WD=Q11387238|lt=Takesusa Shrine|1=Tatehaya Shrine (Takashima)}} * Former Kizu port site * Kizu ancient iron-production site * Shinsei Chemical Industry Shin-Asahi Plant * NPO Kumano-yama Net * Wakamiya Hachiman-sha * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi Shrine (Takashima)|Q11433666|WD=Q11433666|lt=Ōkuninushi Shrine|1=Ookuninushi Shrine (Takashima)}} === Kumano Shrine [Kamikoga, Azumigawa-chō] === {{神社 | 名称=Kumano Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Takashima-kumano2.JPG|250px]]
Kumano Shrine (Kamikoga) honden | 所在地=Kamikoga 1391, Azumigawa-chō, Takashima, Shiga | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|22|06|N|135|58|22|E|}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} | 神事= | 地図={{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} === History === Shrine tradition says it was rebuilt in Kōnin 14. In old times it was called '''Hakusan-gū''', but in Meiji it changed to the present name. In Meiji 9 it was ranked village shrine. === Festival === * Annual festival: May 1 === Nearby === * Genshō-ji * Entsū-ji * Kiyomizu-dera * Hie Shrine * Kamikoga Hachiman Shrine * Azumigawa Koga Post Office === Kumano Shrine [Oi, Imazu-chō] === {{神社 | 名称=Kumano Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Takashima-kumano3.JPG|250px]]
Kumano Shrine (Oi) approach | 所在地=Oi 742, Imazu-chō, Takashima, Shiga | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|24|17|N|135|59|17|E|}} | 祭神=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|WD=Q2531|1=April 25}} | 神事= | 地図={{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === It formerly stood in Nishino village and was moved to the current location in the Eishō era. It is currently outside animal-damage-prevention fencing, so ordinary worship is generally not possible. === Festival === * Annual festival: April 29 === Nearby=== * Saikō-ji * {{ill|lt=Yuge Shrine (Takashima)|Q11487078|WD=Q11487078|lt=Yuge Shrine|1=Yuge Shrine (Takashima)}} * Imazu General Sports Park === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shinto|Q812767|WD=Q812767|1=Shinto}} * {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine|Q3200355|WD=Q3200355|1=Kumano shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kumano Shrine (Q11568963)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kumano Shrine (Q11568963)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]]

Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県高島市新旭町饗庭3363', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '滋賀県高島市安曇川町上古賀', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:熊野神社 (高島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11568963}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年9月}} This article treats '''Kumano Shrine''' (くまのじんじゃ) in {{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|qid=Q845794|1=Takashima, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, which is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}. Although named Kumano Shrine, there is also a tradition that it is unrelated to the so-called {{ill|lt=Kumano Sanzan|Q10403700|qid=Q10403700|1=Kumano Three Shrines}}. Formerly, three major routes ran north-south through Takashima District: the Hokurikudō (Nishi-Ōmi road), Akatsuki (Meigetsu) road, and Saba road. Of these, the Meigetsu road ran across Kumano-yama (today's Aibano upland). It is said Oda Nobunaga used this road during his campaign against the Asakura. Local priestly-family documents (e.g., ''Furumiya Family Documents,'' ''Aiba Family Documents'') record that "the shikinaisha Kumano Shrine originally stood at Nos e in Kumano-yama along Meigetsu road; on March 16, Genki 3, it was burned by Mori Hanbei Tame-mura, and residents moved the sacred symbol into the precinct of {{ill|lt=Harifu Shrine|Q11555577|qid=Q11555577|1=Hanifu Shrine}}." The old site name Mikoshi-daira remains, but it is now within a Self-Defense Force training area and inaccessible. At the former south end (Koga village) and north end (Nishino village) of Meigetsu road were remote worship sites of Kumano Shrine; these correspond to the present Kumano Shrines at Kamikoga in Azumigawa-chō and at Oi in Imazu-chō. === Kumano Shrine [sessha of Harifu Shrine] === {{神社 | 名称=Kumano Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Takashima-kumano1.JPG|250px]]
Kumano Shrine, sessha of Harifu Shrine | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takashima|Q845794|qid=Q845794|1=Takashima, Shiga}}Shin-Asahi-chō Aiba 3363 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E|}} | 祭神=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
sessha of {{ill|lt=Harifu Shrine|Q11555577|qid=Q11555577|1=Hanifu Shrine}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|qid=Q2524|1=April 20}} | 神事= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kumano Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto}} === History === It is said to have been moved into Harifu Shrine's precinct by residents of Kumano-yama in the Tenshō era. Until early Meiji, a Kumano festival was held on June 6 with Noh performances. === Festival === * Annual festival: April 20 === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Takesusa Shrine (Takashima)|Q11387238|qid=Q11387238|lt=Takesusa Shrine|1=Tatehaya Shrine (Takashima)}} * Former Kizu port site * Kizu ancient iron-production site * Shinsei Chemical Industry Shin-Asahi Plant * NPO Kumano-yama Net * Wakamiya Hachiman-sha * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi Shrine (Takashima)|Q11433666|qid=Q11433666|lt=Ōkuninushi Shrine|1=Ookuninushi Shrine (Takashima)}} === Kumano Shrine [Kamikoga, Azumigawa-chō] === {{神社 | 名称=Kumano Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Takashima-kumano2.JPG|250px]]
Kumano Shrine (Kamikoga) honden | 所在地=Kamikoga 1391, Azumigawa-chō, Takashima, Shiga | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|22|06|N|135|58|22|E|}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|qid=Q813858}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|qid=Q682306}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|qid=Q2544|1=May 1}} | 神事= | 地図={{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|qid=Q813858}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|qid=Q682306}} === History === Shrine tradition says it was rebuilt in Kōnin 14. In old times it was called '''Hakusan-gū''', but in Meiji it changed to the present name. In Meiji 9 it was ranked village shrine. === Festival === * Annual festival: May 1 === Nearby === * Genshō-ji * Entsū-ji * Kiyomizu-dera * Hie Shrine * Kamikoga Hachiman Shrine * Azumigawa Koga Post Office === Kumano Shrine [Oi, Imazu-chō] === {{神社 | 名称=Kumano Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Takashima-kumano3.JPG|250px]]

former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭={{ill|lt=April 25|Q2531|qid=Q2531|1=April 25}} | 神事= | 地図={{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|23|07|N|136|01|16|E}}}} }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumano Kusubi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === It formerly stood in Nishino village and was moved to the current location in the Eishō era. It is currently outside animal-damage-prevention fencing, so ordinary worship is generally not possible. === Festival === * Annual festival: April 29 === Nearby=== * Saikō-ji * {{ill|lt=Yuge Shrine (Takashima)|Q11487078|qid=Q11487078|lt=Yuge Shrine|1=Yuge Shrine (Takashima)}} * Imazu General Sports Park === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shinto|Q812767|qid=Q812767|1=Shinto}} * {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine|Q3200355|qid=Q3200355|1=Kumano shrine}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kumano Shrine (Q11568963)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kumano Shrine (Q11568963)]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]]
